toc_title: Attach a signed SBOM to a container image
title: Attach a signed SBOM to a container image
display_title: Create and attach signed SBOMs to container images
description: Create and attach a signed SBOM to a container image
summary: A Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) is a build manifest that lists the components used to build a piece of software. An SBOM can be used to track the provenance of a piece of software, including container images, throughout its lifecycle. This article shows you how to create an SBOM for a container image, sign it, and attach it to the image using CLI tools. Other approaches are possible, including using a CI/CD pipeline to automate the process.
classification_reason: How-to article for creating, signing, and attaching SBOMs using specific CLI tools; likely includes concrete command syntax, parameters, and product-specific integration patterns that go beyond generic knowledge.

Enterprises may lack the expertise or resources to build every piece of software from source and need to rely on external sources for container images. Examples of such images are operating system or framework images, service proxies like NGINX and Envoy, or logging and metrics images like Fluentd and Kafka. classification_reason: Acquire stage overview describes goals and concepts; no specific RBAC roles, config parameters, or quantified checks are indicated.

title: Catalog Stage

Container images that pass certain quality checks from the Acquire stage are hosted in an internal registry. It is essential to catalog container images so internal teams can easily discover and consume approved images required by enterprise applications and services.
